1.content:
Read SESSION_SUMMARY.md and TDD_Telecom_Requirements_AI_System.md to pick up where we left off.

  We are building a Telecom Requirements AI System (Knowledge Graph + RAG). The full design is complete (TDD v0.3). We're ready to start PoC implementation.

  Your role: Applied AI Software Design Partner — collaborate, don't just execute. Decompose before solving, checkpoint progress, surface tradeoffs, push back when needed.

  Next step: PoC Step 1 — Set up Python environment and build the document content extraction layer. Start by installing dependencies (pymupdf pdfplumber python-docx openpyxl olefile Pillow) and extracting text +
  tables from the 5 VZW PDF files in this repo. Build toward the normalized intermediate representation described in TDD Section 5.1.7.

  Clean and simple. The PDFs are in the repo directory, the design docs are there, and the new Claude instance will have everything it needs.

display: Can you make the document parser generic that extracts document format based on few representative docs? This parser shall be able to parse pdf, doc, docx, xl, and xls formats. For example, for Verizon, you can take LTEDATARETRY.pdf and LTEB13NAC.pdf as representative docs. We need to update the design document first. Let me know if you need any clarifications.

Yes, full document format list is: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, and XLSX. I would like the DocumentProfiler to be one time. I would like to make it such that it profiles and creates formats based on representative docs, but updatable by providing more docs, and/or manually updating the generated format itself (which I'm assuming json). Basically, DocumentProfiler itself shall be its own module/executable that gets perfected first (until it gets the document format accurately), before full ingestion pipeline is executed. Actually, the DocumentProfiler shall be standalone, independent of any LLM APIs. For PoC, we will run it on existing Verizon documents. Please update the design document accordingly.
Do git commit with appropriate comments.
OK, now let's start with PoC implementation.
Can you add implementation files to git, and commit with proper comments?

I'm shutting down my PC and would like to continue in an hour. Will you have everything in context tp resume where we are leaving off? Also, update SESSION_SUMMARY.md to reflect our recent conversation and decisions we made, and progress etc.
Can you review the code you generated for first 3 steps and update as needed per the prompt I gave previously you being a senior engineering partner?
[Pasted text #1 +19 lines][Pasted text #2 +19 lines][Pasted text #3 +19 lines]
Update SESSION_SUMMARY.md based on our chat/discussion so far. Then, commit changed files and any new files.
We can skip step-4 for now, and we can update test case related functionality later. Go ahead with step-5.
Which steps you completed so far?

You can re-generate output files when you need in future. Go ahead with step-6. I want to verify whether you got feature toxonomy right or not. So, let me know where and how to check for toxonomy output after completing step-6. Also, modularize and seperate out LLM usage code so that I can plugging our internal LLM APIs later (leave sufficient documentation to do so). Try to use standard APIs (if available) for LLM APIs.
I didn't setup Anthropic API keys yet. Go ahead with mock/stub provider.

Commit all the files (modified and new). Update SESSION_SUMMARY.md to reflect our discussion so far as well as overall progress. Then create a README.md capturing all the details required to test all the steps you completed so far.

Go ahead with Standards ingestion step. Standards ingestion need to make sure to locate, download and extract relevant content based on the references. Can you make this implementation generic (i.e., locating right spec and right version strictly based on how it is referred in the requirement docs in future documents that I will ingest for all MNOs in future) without using LLM APIs?
Go ahead with step-5 (Knowledge graph construction)
Commit all the changed files, new files, and do a push.
Update SESSION_SUMMARY.md with most recent discussion, and progress so that you can resume when I restart my PC.

Sounds good, but make selection of Vector DB, embedding model and associated parameters to be configurable, so that we will have option to play with different options and parameters to find which ones give best performance in terms of accuracy and speed.
Commmit all the changes, git push and continue with next steps.
I'm going to shutdown my PC now and continue later. Did you save everything we discussed in this session, and overall progress?
Read overall session summary from SESSION_SUMMARY.md and continue from where you left off
I want to swap in with a real LLM. However, I want to setup a local model (thinking of Gemmma). Based on my current PC config, which Gemma model do you suggest?
Can you evaluate local Gemma 4 models too for my PC?
Update recent discussions, decisions, and progress in SESSION_SUMMARY.md, commit and push.

We can pause for now. Update README.md with new instructions for local LLM etc. Update SESSION_SUMMARY.md with all our discussion and decisions so far, as well as the progress and next steps so that we can resume later.

We will continue later. For now, update README.md (if needed), design doc (if needed), and SESSION_SUMMARY.md with our discussions/decisions so far in this session. Then commit all changes and push to github.
Read SESSION_SUMMARY.md for all the info on what we have been building, progress and next steps. We will not go through next steps immediately, and wait for my further request.

1. Make pipeline runner scope configurable with arguments --start=1 --end=4 etc. You can even specifiy names of each stage with help argument, and user can pass those names instead of numbers like 1, 2, e etc
2. Yes, please add more configurations with order of best to worst. And have a model picker that fetches HW info ( CPU, GPU, RAM etc) using Linux commands, picks best possible model. However, make model configurable by user if they want to pick their own among available
3. Initial scope is that they provide new documents to verify Document Profiler, document intemediate format, toxanomy, and evaluation q and answer pairs. Actually, I would like to create multiple environments, one for each scope -- Someone will be working on Document Profiler, someone on new eval Q & pairs etc. I will be the admin and create environment for each member. For each environment I should be able to specify document root, what stages to run, scope (single mno single release, single mno multi release etc), objectives (which part of the pipeline and what is being verified). In the document root, there can also be directory for user to supply their Q & A eval pair file (xl?), as well as correction files (e.g., user can "fix" generated toxonamy file afrer first run and re-run the pipeline to generate required compact info that I can enter here for you to fine-tune any logic (for document profiler, ir document, toxanamy) or prompting techniques etc.
4. Agree with your suggestion.
Update README.md, SESSION_SUMMARY.md with all our discussions/decisions and progress. Then commit and push all new and modified files.
I want to change the git info such that it shows authorship as "Mohan" only.
What is the docs directory structure for my requirements documents?
What is the documents directory heirarchy under documents to place multiple releases of multiple Carriers?
Help me come up with name for this project. I named TPM workflow automation project as HILDA (Human In Loop Delivery Automation). I want something similar, catchy, punchy name.
Can you infuse Mobile, Telecom, Network Operators, Requirements, Compliance etc and see what you come up with?
I will go with NORA -- Network Operator Requirements Analyzer

You are an AI/GenAI/Prompt & LLM Engineering expert. We work collaboratively — structured decomposition before solutions, checkpoint-based progress, transparent reasoning, honest about uncertainty. Don't jump to code without aligning on design first. Push back when something doesn't look right.

We are going to build prompt library for SW Development. Each of below phases have a prompt and will be stored as seperate md file.
1) Requirement Gathering, and key design decisions making
2) SW Architecture & Design
3) Development, Testing, & Debugging

We will be using the emotion prompt engineering techniques explained with examples on https://github.com/OuterSpacee/claude-emotion-prompting/tree/main

Crawl above URL, read any papers/documents linked, go through all the samples and create prompts for use-cases I listed previously. You can brainstorm with me on ideas and collaboratively design prompts.

1. Both for personal use and team as well
2. I like structured format, but I like the conciseness of clean prose style. What do you recommend?
3. Idea is that we will be switching between the prompts during overall life-cycle -- Start with 1, then 2, then 3. But in 2 we may go back 2, in 3 we may go back to 2 etc (because we may update design during development, update code during debugging etc). The actual output artifacts (design docs, overall progress maintained in a session summary file etc) can be used to build context for each phase. So, what is your recommendation on for scope per prompt?
4. We start with generic, but we can create domain specific prompts during initiation of project. You can probably create a meta prompt which asks user for domain specific info to create domain specific prompt for each of 3 phases. What do you think?
5. I want to balance comprehensiveness (for better Claude output) and conciseness (to avoid context stuffing that may cause hallucinations). What do you recommend?
6. Can you clarify what you mean here with examples?

I have some feedback on the prompts you generated.
1) For team context, user needs to provide info on contribution structure like who will be contributing and what they will be contributing (full contribution inlcuding design & evelopment, contribution to specific modules/components in terms of their design and development, contributing human validation of artifacts, contribution to correcting output artificats in terms of accuracy and quality that will be fed to LLM to update design or implementation accordingly, contribution to eval data etc). This information needs to be infused in all three prompts as appopriate (e.g., source code and artifact directory structure will need to be organized as per contribution structure).
2) Phas-3 prompt needs to add debug instrumentation so that LLM can analyze the logs user provides during debug session, find root cause and fix. it also needs to consider instrumentation for observability for key performance KPIs like accuracy, RAM/CPU/disk usages (peak & Average), request per second, user response time, LLM API response time etc, that will be monitored and used for potential optimizations (Caching, scaling etc). These KPIs shall be shall stored in a persistent DB.
2) Ask user if LLM has direct access (via file access, email, copy/paste etc) to actual input and eval data sets as well as  output artifacts generated when the project is executed in real environment. If the answer is no, then the development needs to consider our below previous conversaion :

[Pasted text #1 +48 lines]
I would change "Tech Stack" to How we're building
Create git repo for current directory, and push it to https://github.com/kurnoolion
Since I may be creating other types of prompts, can you rename current prompt file names to to indicate they are for SW development?
Create summary of all our discussions and decisions and store in SESSION_SUMMARY.md
let me know the contents of .gitignore
Except for credentials, I want to sync everything else. Why do you want me to not sync Conversation logs, plugins, Session/task/cache data?
OK, I will not sync plugins. Can you create updated .gitignore file? I will just move it over after you produce it.
